title: penstemon cobaea
intro: Penstemon cobaea, also known as the Beard Tongue, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat colds, coughs, and other respiratory problems.
